text:
Paris 2024 Olympic organisers have escaped further embarrassment as the men's and women's triathlon have been saved after the male event was postponed due to water contamination in the River Seine.

The Paris 2024 Olympics triathlon events have been given the go-ahead after a period of concern over contamination in the River Seine.

Organisers confirmed on Wednesday the men’s and women’s triathlons will proceed as recent tests confirmed the water quality has sufficiently improved.

The men’s triathlon had to be delayed from Tuesday due to elevated levels of E. Coli and other harmful bacteria.

This setback required the race to be rescheduled to after the women’s event which will start at 4pm AEST (8am local time).

The organising committee made the announcement at about 4am local time – just four hours before the scheduled start time.

In their brief statement, the World Triathlon organisation declared only, “We will swim” after a spate of criticism following the men’s race delay.

We will swim ðŸ˜¤pic.twitter.com/fJR5UVpn2B

This marks a positive turnaround for Paris 2024, after initial concerns the triathlon might need to be replaced with a duathlon due to the water quality issues.

If the Seine had not met safety standards, the event would have been converted to a duathlon format, substituting the 1500m swim with an additional 5km run.

Heavy rains had previously exacerbated contamination levels, despite substantial investments of more than AU$2 billion to improve water quality in the Seine.

This significant investment had included measures to ensure the triathlon and marathon swimming events could be held in clean waters.

With the triathlon set to go ahead, organisers will continue to cross their fingers for sunny weather ahead of the mixed relay triathlon scheduled for Monday.

The marathon swimming events have also been scheduled for August 8 and 9.

The organising committee will continue to provide updates to ensure athletes and spectators are informed of any changes.

title: Paris 2024 Olympic triathlon saved as green light given by organisers after River Seine contamination clears | Sky News Australia
description: The Paris 2024 Olympics triathlon events have been given the go-ahead after a period of concern over contamination in the River Seine.
